reasoning: The St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index (STLFSI4) has been rising steadily, moving from -0.4433 in late February to -0.1825 for the week ending March 27, 2026. This is being driven by a massive geopolitical crisis (US and Israel escalating conflict with Iran through March) which caused the VIX to spike above 31 by Friday, March 27. STLFSI4 incorporates the VIX and several yield and credit spreads. Credit spreads are reportedly widening, and oil is approaching $100/barrel, causing global stock markets to correct. With weekend news showing Gulf markets continuing to slip and no signs of a ceasefire, the financial stress is firmly in an upward trajectory. Given the momentum of crises to exhibit positive autocorrelation in stress indicators over the short term, the value for the week ending April 3 (the expected comparison date) is highly likely to exceed the March 27 value.
